2605.00893.title: Retrieval-Guided Generation for Safer Histopathology Image Captioning
2605.00893.paper_summary: Generative vision-language models can produce fluent medical image captions but remain prone to hallucination, over-specific diagnostic claims, and factual inconsistency-serious issues in pathology. We investigate retrieval-guided generation (RGG) as a safer alternative, where captions are formed by summarizing expert text from visually similar cases rather than generated de novo. On the ARCH histopathology dataset, RGG improves semantic alignment with ground truth, achieving cosine similarity of $\approx$0.60 versus $\approx$0.47 from MedGemma, with non-overlapping confidence intervals indicating a robust gain. A pathologist-led qualitative review shows better preservation of morphology-relevant terminology and fewer unsupported diagnoses, while revealing failure modes such as concept mixing and inherited over-specific labeling. Overall, retrieval-guided captioning offers a more transparent and reliable approach with clearer opportunities for auditing than fully generative methods.
2605.00893.paper_summary_zh: 生成式視覺-語言模型能夠產生流暢的醫學影像標題，但仍然容易出現幻覺、過於具體的診斷主張以及事實不一致——這在病理學中是嚴重的問題。我們研究檢索引導生成（RGG）作為一種更安全的替代方案，在這種方案中，標題是通過總結來自視覺上相似案例的專家文本而形成的，而不是全新生成的。在ARCH組織病理學數據集中，RGG提高了與真實情況的語義對齊，達到約0.60的餘弦相似度，而MedGemma則為約0.47，且不重疊的置信區間顯示出穩健的增益。一項由病理學家主導的定性評估顯示，在形態學相關術語的保留上表現更佳，且不支持的診斷較少，同時揭示了概念混合和繼承的過於具體標籤等失敗模式。總體而言，檢索引導的標題生成提供了一種更透明和可靠的方法，並且相比於完全生成的方法，具有更清晰的審核機會。
2605.00893.author: Md. Enamul Hoq et.al.
2605.00893.authors: Md. Enamul Hoq, Wataru Uegami, Saghir Alfasly, Ghazal Alabtah, Sahar Rahimi Malakshan, Armita Kazemi, Alex T. Schmitgen, Fred Prior, H. R. Tizhoosh
